hook.text: A team shipped a broken build on Friday afternoon. The fix took all weekend. Morale cratered.

buildup.text: Without a pipeline, quality depends on memory. Did someone run the tests? Did someone check the config?

discovery.text: Pipelines enforce consistency. Every commit gets the same build, test, and scan — no human shortcuts.

twist.text: Speed follows consistency. Teams with pipelines deploy 200x more often than those without.

climax.text: Pipelines also create audit trails. Regulators can see exactly what was tested and when.

punchline.text: Pipelines enforce quality and enable speed. Both at once.

quiz.question: How do pipelines improve deployment consistency?
options: Every commit gets the same automated build and test process
They remind developers to test manually
They slow down releases to ensure quality
